## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 The minimum processing time of a Schengen visa application is 15 calendar days. It can take up to 45 days for Schengen Visa and up to 90 days for National Visa.
Applicants can apply until 6 months before their travel dates with the exception of the seafarers, in the performance of their duties, no more than 9 months in advance to the intended travel date.
One cannot apply if there are less than 15 days before their travel date, meaning that they cannot apply if there are less than 15 calendar days, between the application reaches the Consulate, and their travel date. Please kindly note that this rule is totally independent from the processing time which depends on applicants’ nationalities and, in case of incomplete documents submitted, starts only when the Consulate receives all the necessary documents, if considered correct.
Please be aware that it is not possible to expedited processing time because the system is completely automated.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).
Applicants also need to be aware that, especially during holiday seasons (i.e. Easter, Summer, Christmas), the appointments (see Schedule an Appointment) are being booked very quickly as the Mission only processes a fixed amount of visa applications on a daily basis.
Furthermore, applicants need to consider that VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays (see Public Holidays/Closures) may extend the visa application processing time.
Therefore, VFS recommends all applicants:
- to book their appointment as soon as they know they’ll be travelling;
- to ensure that their visa application’s documentation meets the Mission’s requirements;
- to make sure the minimum time eligibility is respected.

visa_types.name: Medical Treatment
visa_types.documents_required:
Checklist for Medical Treatment
- Visa application form, filled completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) + 2 photocopies of pages bearing particulars of the applicant of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants' usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Introduction letter from employer or school + parental consent (if applicant under 18 years)
- International health insurance cover for the duration of stay/medical insurance
- Flight booking slip - Reservation of the return ticket bill or proof of the availability of personal means of transport
- Air-ticket (open) for only applicants of long stay visa + 1 photocopy
Medical documentation  including
- Medical documentation established by the country of origin attesting disease of the applicant.
- Declaration of a public or private Italian hospital (having to be accredited by the National Service of Health) indicating the type of the treatment, the date estimated, duration and the cost.
- Document of the Italian hospital confirming the deposit from at least 30% of the estimated cost of the necessary treatment or a specific administrative certificate by an Italian area or a specific authorization by the Ministry for Health sponsoring a program of humanitarian help
- Document proving the availability in Italy of financial resources sufficient in Italy
Clarification on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017
- It is considered necessary to specify the following:The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence of both parents, by a person in possession of a notarized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, procede as followed;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, proceed as followed;

- In case of family reunion application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the issuance of the authorization (Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office (via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admissible and therefore not accepted. The principles referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
People lawfully residing in Italy, having lost their residence permit or people having left the country before receiving the renewal of the said permit, can apply for the re-entry visa
                                                                        proving that he/she has travelled out of the country for genuine and importatnt reasons.
The visa will be issued from the competent "Questura" (Police Headquarters) in Italy. It is a long term period visa (type D) - Single entry.

## Visa Fees

(*) The fee will be applicable per set of documents to be legalized (each set will contain a maximum of 4 documents, (i.e) 2 originals and 2 translations). From the fifth document, the
                                                                        fee will be applicable twice.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Re-entry Visa
- Visa application form, filled completely in 
block letters,and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) + 2 photocopies of pages bearing particulars of the applicant of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants' usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- In the case of a theft or a loss of residence permit, a copy of the report of loss or theft of the local police force translated into Italian
- In the case of the expiry of a residence permit within a time lower than 60 days, the original or the copy of the expired document and a copy of the receipt of request for renewal
Clarification on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017

It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence of both parents, by a person in possession of a notarized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, proceed as followed;

- In case of family reunion application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the issuance of the authorization (Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office (via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admissible and therefore not accepted. The principles referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
The minimum processing time of a Schengen visa application is 15 calendar days. It can take up to 45 days for Schengen Visa and up to 90 days for National Visa.
Applicants can apply until 6 months before their travel dates with the exception of the seafarers, in the performance of their duties, no more than 9 months in advance to the intended travel date.
One cannot apply if there are less than 15 days before their travel date, meaning that they cannot apply if there are less than 15 calendar days, between the application reaches the Consulate, and their travel date. Please kindly note that this rule is totally independent from the processing time which depends on applicants’ nationalities and, in case of incomplete documents submitted, starts only when the Consulate receives all the necessary documents, if considered correct.
Please be aware that it is not possible to expedited processing time because the system is completely automated.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).
Applicants also need to be aware that, especially during holiday seasons (i.e. Easter, Summer, Christmas), the appointments (see Schedule an Appointment) are being booked very quickly as the Mission only processes a fixed amount of visa applications on a daily basis.
Furthermore, applicants need to consider that VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays (see Public Holidays/Closures) may extend the visa application processing time.
Therefore, VFS recommends all applicants:
- to book their appointment as soon as they know they’ll be travelling;
- to ensure that their visa application’s documentation meets the Mission’s requirements;
- to make sure the minimum time eligibility is respected.

## Overview
The Visa allows applicants to enter Italy for a short term period (type "C") or for a long term period (type "D") with the purpose to work while earning a salary.
This Visas are subject to an Authorisation ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authority ("SPORTELLO UNICO PER L'IMMIGRAZIONE") which is valid within 6 months from the date
                                                                        of issuing.

## Visa Fees

                                                                        fee will be applicable twice.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Subordinate Employment Visa
- Visa application form, filled completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +2 photocopy of the bio data page of the said passport
- Previous passports, (if any) + 2 photocopies of pages bearing particulars of the applicant 
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants' usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Original Birth Certificate of Applicant
- Original and one copy of "Nulla Osta" delivered not more than 6 months by "Sportello Unico per l’Immigrazione".

- Original Declaration of Family Status to include details of ALL persons in nuclear family - To be done by the public notary or the High Court - by the applicant, and not by a relative. If a bachelor and without children, it should be stated clearly in the declaration.

Original Ordinance Marriage Certificate and one clear copy
Copy of birth certificate of spouse with one passport pictures attached.
- If any children;
Original birth certificate of child with one clear photocopy and one passport picture attached
Clarification on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017

It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence of both parents, by a person in possession of a notarized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, proceed as followed;

- In case of family reunion application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the issuance of the authorization (Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office (via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admissible and therefore not accepted. The principles referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment. rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 The minimum processing time of a Schengen visa application is 15 calendar days. It can take up to 45 days for Schengen Visa and up to 90 days for National Visa.
Applicants can apply until 6 months before their travel dates with the exception of the seafarers, in the performance of their duties, no more than 9 months in advance to the intended travel date.
One cannot apply if there are less than 15 days before their travel date, meaning that they cannot apply if there are less than 15 calendar days, between the application reaches the Consulate, and their travel date. Please kindly note that this rule is totally independent from the processing time which depends on applicants’ nationalities and, in case of incomplete documents submitted, starts only when the Consulate receives all the necessary documents, if considered correct.
Please be aware that it is not possible to expedited processing time because the system is completely automated.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).
Applicants also need to be aware that, especially during holiday seasons (i.e. Easter, Summer, Christmas), the appointments (see Schedule an Appointment) are being booked very quickly as the Mission only processes a fixed amount of visa applications on a daily basis.
Furthermore, applicants need to consider that VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays (see Public Holidays/Closures) may extend the visa application processing time.
Therefore, VFS recommends all applicants:
- to book their appointment as soon as they know they’ll be travelling;
- to ensure that their visa application’s documentation meets the Mission’s requirements;
to make sure the minimum time eligibility is respected.

visa_types.documents_required:
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR FAMILY REUNION APPLICATION.
GENERAL MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS
Long Stay Visa form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/ghana/english/pdf/Long-Stay-Italy-141214.pdf) duly filled for each applicant.

4 Passport pictures (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/ghana/english/#Family) of each applicant on a white background; one pasted on, and the other three attached to Visa form
2 Clear and Legible copy of Bio Data pages of passport for each applicant.
Original passport of applicant. The passport must have a validity of at least 6 months from departure date.
Original nulla osta.
Original legalised birth certificate and one clear copy for each applicant.
Original and recent certificate of residenza e stato di famiglia of relation in italy, valid within six months of application.
ORIGINAL sworn Declaration of Family Status of relation in Italy (in Italian “ATTO NOTORIO”) including details of ALL Family member both in Italy and abroad.
Clear copy of old and new “permesso di soggiorno” of relation in italy.
Clear copy of valid “carta d’identità” of relation in italy.
Clear copy of all pages of all passports of relation in italy.
One passport picture of relation in italy.
Copy of birth certificate of relation in italy.
- SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS FOR SPOUSE:
Original Affidavit including details of ALL family members – To be done at the Public Notary or the High Court – by the applicant in Ghana.
Original Legalised Ordinance Marriage Certificate and One clear copy.
ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS FOR SPOUSE:
Ordinance Marriage Pictures, and/or other pictures of the couple.
Copy of verifiable Money Transfers/Remittance with the name of relation in Italy and the applicant.
Any other useful document to support the genuinity of the relationship.
- SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS FOR CHILDREN;
Original guardian’s permission to travel – please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4G46e2YBoFP1fPsMdoNaFj/466019ff30c961bdb8cd1a9acc482d05/consent-vfs.pdf) for more information.
Original school certificates and report cards of the child.
ORIGINAL Weighing Card of Child.
For those who are lacking proper certification to prove the paternity/maternity of relation in Italy, it is possible to voluntarily undergo a DNA test, following the OIM Procedure.
- SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS FOR PARENT(S);
Original Affidavit Including details of ALL family members (in Ghana and abroad) – to be done at the Public Notary or the High Court – by the applicant in Ghana.
Copy of verifiable Money Transfers/Remittance with the name of relation in Italy and the applicant, for a period of at least 3 years.
Please Note that ALL documents must be presented at Visa appointment at VFS.
Failure to submit complete and consistent application may cause delay in the Visa process or a denial.
Starting from 11 October 2024, no rejection notice will be issued by the Embassy, therefore it will not be possible to submit additional documents after Visa appointment.
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR FAMILY REUNION WITH ITALIAN NATIONAL.

GENERAL REQUIREMENTS;
- Long Stay Visa form duly filled for applicant.
- 4 Passport pictures of each applicant on a white background; two pasted on forms 1 and 2 and the other two attached to the Visa form
- 1 Clear and Legible copy each of Data pages of passport for each applicant.
- ORIGINAL passport of applicant.
- ORIGINAL Birth Certificate and ONE Clear copy for each applicant.
- ORIGINAL and RECENT Stato di Famiglia of relation in Italy, valid within SIX months of application.
- ORIGINAL Certificato Residenza Storico Anagrafico of relation in Italy valid within SIX months of application.
- ORIGINAL Declaration of Family Status of relation in Italy (ATTO NOTORIO) to include details of ALL persons in Nuclear Family.
- Clear copy of OLD Permesso di Soggiorno of relation in Italy.
- Clear copy of VALID Carta D’Identita of relation in Italy.
- Clear copy of ALL pages of ALL GHANAIAN passports ever possessed of relation in Italy.
- Clear copy of CERTIFICATO DI CITTADINANZA of relation in Italy.
IF SPOUSE;
- ORIGINAL Declaration of Family Status to include details of ALL persons in Nuclear Family – To be done at the Public Notary or the High Court – by Spouse in Ghana.
- ORIGINAL LEGALISED Ordinance Marriage Certificate and One clear copy.
- Proof of registered Marriage with the embassy by Italian Spouse.
- Certificato di Stato Libero of Spouse in Italy.
FOR CHILDREN;
- ORIGINAL Guardian’s Permission to Travel – To be done at the Public Notary or the High Court – And copy of Photo Identity Documents and ONE passport pictures. Please
 
click here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/ghana/english/pdf/consent-to-expatriation-vfs.pdf)
 
to view the consent declaration from parents of minors.
- ORIGINAL TESTIMONIAL and Copy of Class Register.
- ORIGINAL B.E.C and/or W.A.S.S.C with one clear copy each, if applicant has completed J.H.S and/or S.H.S.
- ORIGINAL Weighing Card of Child.
FOR PARENT(S);
- ORIGINAL Declaration of Family Status by Parent in Ghana to include details of ALL children both in and outside the country.
- ORIGINALS of Money transfer Receipts received from Child in Italy.
- Clear copy of Birth Certificate of Child – Italian Citizen – in Italy.

                                                                        fee will be applicable twice.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Cultural Visa
- Visa application form, filled completely in block letters, and signed by the applicant.
- Signed Passport (valid for at least 3 months longer than intended stay), +1 photo copy of the said passport (without the blank pages)
- Previous passports, (if any) + 2 photocopies of pages bearing particulars of the applicant of pages bearing particulars of the applicant
- 2 recent passport size photographs (not more than 3 months old) with applicants usual signature at the back
- Visa Fee + VFS Charges

- International health insurance cover for the duration of stay

Proof of economic ties with Ghana (+ 2 photocopies of pages bearing particulars of the applicant)

- Other incomes

Cultural

- Introductory letter from an association and musician card and proof of musical activities.
- For recording: studio contract & transfer slip for instalments of studio fees.
- For lessons: contract stating duration and payment.
For festivals or concerts: detailed concert schedule, contract stating duration and payment
Clarification on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017
- It is considered necessary to specify the following:The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence of both parents, by a person in possession of a notarized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, proceed as followed;
- In case of family reunion application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the issuance of the authorization (Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office (via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admissible and therefore not accepted. The principles referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
 The minimum processing time of a Schengen visa application is 15 calendar days. It can take up to 45 days for Schengen Visa and up to 90 days for National Visa.
Applicants can apply until 6 months before their travel dates with the exception of the seafarers, in the performance of their duties, no more than 9 months in advance to the intended travel date.
One cannot apply if there are less than 15 days before their travel date, meaning that they cannot apply if there are less than 15 calendar days, between the application reaches the Consulate, and their travel date. Please kindly note that this rule is totally independent from the processing time which depends on applicants’ nationalities and, in case of incomplete documents submitted, starts only when the Consulate receives all the necessary documents, if considered correct.
Please be aware that it is not possible to expedited processing time because the system is completely automated.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).
Applicants also need to be aware that, especially during holiday seasons (i.e. Easter, Summer, Christmas), the appointments (see Schedule an Appointment) are being booked very quickly as the Mission only processes a fixed amount of visa applications on a daily basis.
Furthermore, applicants need to consider that VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays (see Public Holidays/Closures) may extend the visa application processing time.
Therefore, VFS recommends all applicants:
- to book their appointment as soon as they know they’ll be travelling;
- to ensure that their visa application’s documentation meets the Mission’s requirements;
- to make sure the minimum time eligibility is respected.

Clarification on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at least one parent.

It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence of both parents, by a person in possession of a notarized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, proceed as followed

- In case of family reunion application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the issuance of the authorization (Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office (via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.
It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admissible and therefore not accepted. The principles referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
The minimum processing time of a Schengen visa application is 15 calendar days. It can take up to 45 days for Schengen Visa and up to 90 days for National Visa.
Applicants can apply until 6 months before their travel dates with the exception of the seafarers, in the performance of their duties, no more than 9 months in advance to the intended travel date.
One cannot apply if there are less than 15 days before their travel date, meaning that they cannot apply if there are less than 15 calendar days, between the application reaches the Consulate, and their travel date. Please kindly note that this rule is totally independent from the processing time which depends on applicants’ nationalities and, in case of incomplete documents submitted, starts only when the Consulate receives all the necessary documents, if considered correct.
Please be aware that it is not possible to expedited processing time because the system is completely automated.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).
Applicants also need to be aware that, especially during holiday seasons (i.e. Easter, Summer, Christmas), the appointments (see Schedule an Appointment) are being booked very quickly as the Mission only processes a fixed amount of visa applications on a daily basis.
Furthermore, applicants need to consider that VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays (see Public Holidays/Closures) may extend the visa application processing time.
Therefore, VFS recommends all applicants:
- to book their appointment as soon as they know they’ll be travelling;
- to ensure that their visa application’s documentation meets the Mission’s requirements;
- to make sure the minimum time eligibility is respected.

visa_types.name: Visa Types
visa_types.overview:
Schengen Visa Information:
 A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before applying at the Italy Visa Application Centre in Ghana.

In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please apply to the Embassy of the country where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the number of days to be spent in multiple countries is the same, you must apply to the embassy of the country which will be your first point of entry.

If you plan to apply for a Schengen visa at the Italy Visa Application Centre, your main destination and the maximum period of stay in the Schengen area must be mainly in Italy. Please provide proof to support this information.
visa_types.full_text:
## Schengen Visa Information:
 A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before applying at the Italy Visa Application Centre in Ghana.

In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please apply to the Embassy of the country where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the number of days to be spent in multiple countries is the same, you must apply to the embassy of the country which will be your first point of entry.

If you plan to apply for a Schengen visa at the Italy Visa Application Centre, your main destination and the maximum period of stay in the Schengen area must be mainly in Italy. Please provide proof to support this information.

## Required Travel Insurance
A medical insurance is compulsory for visitors to Italy and other Schengen states. The insurance must cover at least 30 000 Euros and include costs for medical treatment, hospitalization and repatriation due to injury or medical reasons.

The reimbursement from the insurance company must be payable in any EU-country, Switzerland and Lichtenstein.

The company issuing the insurance can be based anywhere but preferably you should use a company base in your home country or the country you are travelling to. The Embassy must asses whether the company is solid or not. For this purpose all Schengen Missions in Ghana have compiled a list of trusted companies. If you use a company that has not been assessed by the Schengen Missions the handling time of your application will be at least 30 days.

Please note that you have to bring your travel insurance with you when travelling due to inspections of relevant documents at the border control when entering the Schengen area.

- Evidence of the registered business in Ghana (original certificates including:FormC,Form3 and Companies Code
- VAT Registration, tax clearance certificate and bills of lading
- Invitation, pro-forma invoice,
- certificate of business registration (with Italian Chamber of Commerce)
- Original correspondence with your business contact in Italy
- Original letter indicating designation and authority to act on behalf of company
- Bank statements (personal and company) for last 3 months (+ 2 photocopies of pages bearing particulars of the applicant)
- Reservation for accommodation (Hotel Reservation)
- Recent copy of the Register of Trade (« Visura camerale ») from the Italian company
Clarification on the acceptance of visa applications of minors who are not accompanied by at least one parent.
ACCRA – 31 January 2017

It is considered necessary to specify the following:
- The visa applications for minors (for which ever type of visa) can be submitted and undersigned only by one of the parents or, in the absence of one of the parents or, in the absence of both parents, by a person in possession of a notarized authorization attesting the identity and relation as well as the contacts and address for purpose of any notification;
- In the case a minor’s application is presented in the absence of a parent or a formal delegation, proceed as followed;

- In case of family reunion application type D, a new appointment date can be given in queue with already fixed appointments. If the new interview date goes beyond the 180 days of the issuance of the authorization (Nulla Osta), the case should be notified to the Visa Office (via E-mail);
- In case of a type C visa application, the applicant will be invited to take contact again when in possession of the requested documentation.

It should be noted that applications not complete with the required translated and legalized documents or Passports will not be considered admissible and therefore not accepted. The principles referred in point b) must be applied for the rescheduling of the appointment.

- Visa Fee + VFS Charges
- Introduction letter from employer or school + parental consent (if applicant under 18 years)
- International health insurance cover for the duration of stay
- Flight booking slip - Reservation of the bill return ticket or bill or proof of the availability of personal means of transport

## Photo Specifications
Photographs 
Please provide your recent photograph (not more than 6 months old). The photographs should be in colour: - 
- Taken against a clear background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background;
- Clear and of good quality;
- Printed on normal photographic paper;
- Unmounted - 45 mm x 35 mm in size;
- Full face (without sunglasses and normally without a hat or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).

## Processing Times
The minimum processing time of a Schengen visa application is 15 calendar days. It can take up to 45 days for Schengen Visa and up to 90 days for National Visa.
Applicants can apply until 6 months before their travel dates with the exception of the seafarers, in the performance of their duties, no more than 9 months in advance to the intended travel date.
One cannot apply if there are less than 15 days before their travel date, meaning that they cannot apply if there are less than 15 calendar days, between the application reaches the Consulate, and their travel date. Please kindly note that this rule is totally independent from the processing time which depends on applicants’ nationalities and, in case of incomplete documents submitted, starts only when the Consulate receives all the necessary documents, if considered correct.
Please be aware that it is not possible to expedited processing time because the system is completely automated.
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete and its documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements (see Documents Required for the different visa categories).
Applicants also need to be aware that, especially during holiday seasons (i.e. Easter, Summer, Christmas), the appointments (see Schedule an Appointment) are being booked very quickly as the Mission only processes a fixed amount of visa applications on a daily basis.
Furthermore, applicants need to consider that VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays (see Public Holidays/Closures) may extend the visa application processing time.
Therefore, VFS recommends all applicants:
- to book their appointment as soon as they know they’ll be travelling;
- to ensure that their visa application’s documentation meets the Mission’s requirements;
- to make sure the minimum time eligibility is respected.
